title: Passing a reversed iterable to Set, sorted(), or reversed() should be avoided
description: Using a reversed iterable with functions that require an iterable in sorted order can lead to unexpected behavior and performance issues.
message: A reversed iterable was passed to Set, sorted(), or reversed() function.
recommendation: Use a list and reverse() function instead.

noncompliant.description: Creates a set with reversed elements

compliant.description: Creates a set with elements in order
Creates a set with reversed elements and reverses the list before adding

why_is_this_an_issue: Using a reversed iterable can result in unexpected behavior and performance issues when the function expects the iterable to be in sorted order.
how_to_fix: Use a list and reverse() function instead.

metadata.title: Passing a reversed iterable to Set, sorted(), or reversed() should be avoided
